behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification quality on explicit imagery. A stubbed classifier can only confirm routing logic, not whether explicit images are actually detected. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is a single model judgment plus hold decision, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a labeled set of real explicit and non-explicit images through the moderation service with the production vision model enabled; assert explicit images are flagged and persisted in review queue and never reach publish path.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model robustness to obfuscation variants; deterministic fixtures hide this failure. Rejected Workflow: this is one agentic classification capability, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of hate-speech samples with obfuscation transformations (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through the real text moderation model; assert they are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The core risk is module handoff/orchestration: when both checks return pass, publish state must propagate correctly and not be dropped. Direct quote disqualifying higher level: "passes both content checks" makes this assertion about post-check plumbing, not model-output quality. Rejected System: no required real OS/network behavior is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator + persistence/queue + publisher with deterministic pass responses from image/text check modules; assert final state is published, not held, and record exists in delivery path (no silent drop).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower than Agentic is justified because assertion excludes evaluation of model correctness and tests only routing/state propagation.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (image model + text model + decision) with an end-to-end latency SLA. Mocking either model hides real timing and interaction failures. Rejected pure System: behavior text requires the full multi-agentic pipeline, not just infrastructure timing.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end submissions through real image and text moderation models under representative peak-hour load; measure submission-to-decision latency and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <=5s across the full chain.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This tests failure-handling plumbing across modules (model client error -> retry queue -> no auto-approve). A controlled unavailable response can reproduce the failure without real network/OS primitives. Rejected System: behavior text says "model is unavailable" but does not require validating real network timeout semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Inject deterministic vision-client unavailability errors (e.g., timeout/503) in a wired moderation service with real retry queue component; assert image posts enter retry queue and publish flag remains false.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If production incidents suggest transport-specific failures (DNS/TLS/socket), elevate to System.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency for borderline content depends on real model behavior (and any stochasticity/calibration), which mocks cannot expose. Rejected Workflow: repeated submissions test consistency of one agentic decision type rather than a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline images repeatedly within the target time window through the real vision moderation path; assert decision consistency rate meets threshold and investigate drift/flip patterns.
